name: Nord Stream 1+2 pipeline explosions
notes: Four leaks across NS1 + NS2; Swedish investigators found explosive traces. Sweden and Denmark closed investigations in 2024 without naming suspects. Most cinematically important Baltic seabed-warfare event.

name: Balticconnector pipeline + EE-FI telecom cable cut
notes: Finnish investigators determined dragged anchor caused damage; anchor recovered at pipeline site. Ship's captain faced Hong Kong court charges. Damage occurred over Oct 7-8 window across multiple cables. Pipeline closed for repairs ~6 months.

name: BCS East-West cable cut (Yi Peng 3)
notes: First of two cables damaged on consecutive days. Yi Peng 3 anchored in Kattegat for weeks under multi-national investigation. Swedish inquiry found no conclusive evidence of deliberate anchor dragging; case did not yield criminal charges.

name: C-Lion1 Helsinki-Rostock cable cut (Yi Peng 3)
notes: Second cable damaged ~18 hours after BCS East-West; same vessel suspected. Track suggested anchor-drag pattern. Same Swedish inquiry as INC-2024-11-17 found no conclusive deliberate-intent evidence.

name: Estlink 2 power cable + 4 telecom cables cut (Eagle S)
notes: Hero demo candidate. Russian shadow-fleet linked tanker. Finnish authorities boarded and detained vessel on the day. Estlink 2 down for months. Anchor recovered. Finnish court DISMISSED case in October 2025; prosecutors failed to prove intent. Behavioral pattern (anchor drag over cable) is exactly what our model should catch regardless of legal outcome.

name: Latvia-Sweden (Ventspils-Gotland) cable cut (Vezhen)
notes: Cable severed at ~100m depth in Swedish EEZ. Vezhen had departed Russian port of Ust-Luga days earlier. Sweden detained vessel; Swedish prosecutor later ruled breach UNINTENTIONAL and released vessel. Triggered NATO Baltic Sentry mission launched Jan 14 2025 (delayed announcement).

name: Germany-Finland data cable damage (Cinia)
notes: Cinia announced damage Feb 21 2025; sabotage suspected at location east of Gotland. No vessel identified as suspect in public reporting. May or may not overlap with C-Lion1 route.

name: Finland-Estonia Elisa data cable damage (Fitburg)
notes: Elisa noticed disruption at 04:53 EET (02:53 UTC). Vessel en route Saint Petersburg to Haifa. Finnish police found anchor and chain had dragged tens of km along seabed. Detained in Kantvik port; released 2026-01-12. 14 crew from Russia/Georgia/Kazakhstan/Azerbaijan. Russian structural steel (EU-sanctioned) found onboard. Damage may extend to Estonia-Sweden cable per Bairdmaritime.

name: Lithuania-Latvia (Sventoji-Liepaja) cable damage
notes: Subsea telecom cable linking Sventoji (LT) and Liepaja (LV) damaged Jan 2 2026. Latvian police later boarded a vessel and initiated criminal proceedings but found no evidence linking to damage. Pattern of repeated unattributed incidents continues post-NATO-Baltic-Sentry.
